Empires & Puzzles Gafar Review: Is The Sand Vizier Still Worth It?
Dust off the Vizier! Our in-depth review of Gafar explores if this fast sniper and healing-blocker from the Sand Empire still has a place in your roster or if he belongs in the hourglass of history.
Gafar: The 4-Star Sniper Who Hates Healers
Gafar, The Sand Vizier: Dust, Decay, and Death to Healers
Gafar is a classic 4-star Dark (Purple) hero from the
seasonal event. Originally released way back when dinosaurs roamed the match-3 board, he was once a premier 4-star sniper known for his ability to completely shut down enemy healers.

, he has the potential to resist mana-stopping ailments, which pairs nicely with his fast mana speed. He serves as a "glass cannon" sniper—hitting quickly and disrupting healing, but shattering if you look at him the wrong way.

Gafar’s design is a clear nod to the classic "evil vizier" trope (looking at you, Jafar). Clad in desert robes with a stern, bearded visage and wielding a golden snake staff, he exudes authority and malice. The art style is distinctly from the earlier era of Empires & Puzzles—less flashy particle effects, more "painted portrait" style. It’s a solid, thematic look that tells you exactly who he is: a guy who probably plots to overthrow the Sultan in his spare time.
Let’s be brutally honest here: Gafar’s stats are showing their age.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Sand Empire heroes are from an older generation, so their kit is simpler. They lack the complex passives of the "Construct" or "Astral" families.

The Sand Empire family bonus boosts defense. This is critical for Gafar. Pairing him with another Sand hero (like the 3-star Hisan or 5-star Rana) gives him a much-needed survivability bump. It’s not flashy, but it helps patch his biggest weakness.
Gafar comes from an era before passives were handed out like candy. He has
. What you see is what you get.

is straightforward. It boosts his snipe and his tile damage. While a defensive Aether power might have helped him survive, leaning into his role as a damage dealer is fine. It helps ensure his "Sand Gale" actually leaves a mark.
When Gafar was released, he was a top-tier 4-star. Field Aid wars (where defenses healed automatically) were his playground. He was the answer to the annoying Rigard/Boril tanks of 2018.
The game has moved on. His stats are now comparable to a well-trained 3-star. His damage multiplier hasn't kept pace with the massive health pools of newer heroes.
He is no longer a general-purpose hero. You cannot bring him into a standard Diamond Arena raid and expect him to survive. However, he retains specific viability in
(especially Buff Booster or Rush where healers are prevalent) and specifically for the
Note: This review focuses on the base version of Gafar.

While Gafar does have a costume in the live game that updates his stats and changes his class, the base version reviewed here suffers significantly from "stat rot." The costume is almost a mandatory upgrade to bring his stats even remotely close to modern standards. Without the costume bonus, base Gafar is extremely fragile.
Because Gafar is made of glass, you have a difficult choice: try to fix his weakness or go all-in on his strength.
Maximizes his tile damage and snipe.
He will die if a stiff breeze hits him. Not recommended for modern play.
Pushes his defense closer to the 700 mark and health over 1100. This might let him survive one slash attack or a stray special skill.
Hits softer, but a dead Gafar deals 0 damage.
path is mandatory. Gafar’s utility is his healing block. He needs to survive long enough to cast it. Dead snipers don't stop heals.
-100% healing is still one of the most potent debuffs in the game.
He can charge and fire before the slow/average healers on the enemy team.
Immune to attack drops or blind if he can fire his skill.
High base attack for a 4-star makes his purple tiles hurt.
His defensive stats are ancient. He is easily one-shot by modern snipers.
While he stops healing, a monk resisting the ailment or a cleanser firing
the heal can negate his primary function.
He effectively has 3.5-star stats in a 5-star world.
Specifically Bloody Battle? No, healing is already blocked there.
are his homes. In Rush, he can shut down the enemy healers before they undo your work.
If you're facing a team with a pesky healer left alive, Gafar is a great surgical tool to ensure they don't recover.
The Cleric trial is notoriously short on damage dealers. Gafar shines here simply because he can actually hit things.
Decent tile damage for hitting yellow Titans if you lack better options like Sergei or Tiburtus.
The key to using Gafar is protection. You must shield him so he can deliver his payload.
Mist slows mana and drops special defense, Proteus stops mana entirely, and Gafar ensures that even if they get mana, they can't heal. It’s a control-heavy team.
Tiburtus drops defense, Sergei buffs purple damage, and Gafar hits (harder).
(Rigard, if he fires without healing first, or other cleansers).
Don't pair him with other healing blockers if you can avoid it; the effects don't stack, they just overwrite.

If you pull Gafar early, he is a fantastic asset. His high attack will carry you through map stages, and his ability to stop bosses from healing is invaluable in Season 1 and 2. Max him out, but be wary of spending too many emblems unless he is your main sniper.

He is likely a bench warmer for you. Unless you are competing hard in 4-star tournaments or have a deep roster need for War Cleanups, he probably isn't worth the ascension materials compared to modern 4-stars. Do
Limit Break him unless you have his costume and plan to use him competitively in tournaments.
The community has always had a soft spot for the Sandman, mostly due to his high attack (for the time).
“I used him everywhere, didn’t care much about his cons, he turned the tide countless times in field aid wars.”
“He’s very useful actually. It’s between him and Stonecleave and stone lost the toss.”
— A sentiment from the past that still holds up; Gafar is generally more reliable than Stonecleave.

Gafar is a relic of a simpler time. He does one thing—stops healing—and he does it fast. While his stats have crumbled to dust over the years, that specific utility keeps him hanging onto the edge of relevance by his fingernails. He’s not the terrifying Vizier he once was, but in the right tournament, he can still ruin a healer's day.
A situational tool for tournaments, but too fragile for the main stage. Keep him for the depth, but don't build your empire on this sand.
A comprehensive review of Gafar, the 4-star Dark hero from the Sand Empire event. We analyze his stats, special skill, and viability in the modern Empires & Puzzles meta.
